words stack upward — visually builds from the ground up.

Dynamic rise distance: each row rises from proportional depth below base

Bottom row rises 40px, top row rises (N-1)*50+40px — words physically
travel from the foundation level up to their final position.

Increase towerRowIn translateY for more dramatic rise from base

instead of barely-noticeable 20px nudge.

animation) win ties over observation deck rows. Big/prominent words now
land at the foundation and visibly appear first as the tower builds up.

Make tower build animation visibly bottom-to-top

Increase row stagger from 40ms to 100ms and reduce per-row duration
from 500ms to 400ms, making the cascading bottom-to-top build effect
clearly perceivable (~1.1s total for 8 rows).

commit.message:
fix: use regional QStash URL (qstash-us-east-1.upstash.io)

QStash client was defaulting to qstash.upstash.io which returns
"user not found" for us-east-1 accounts. Now explicitly reads
QSTASH_URL env var with .trim() to handle trailing whitespace.
Also removes dead effectiveUserId code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add use_library flag to use liked songs as taste profile

When use_library=true, fetches a random sample of 50 liked songs from
the user's Spotify library and includes them as taste context in both
the query generation and curation AI prompts. This helps Claude generate
playlists that match the user's actual listening preferences instead of
guessing blindly.

Changes:
- Add user-library-read to Spotify OAuth scopes
- Add SpotifyClient.get_liked_tracks() (2 API calls: count + random slice)
- Add format_library_context() helper
- Add library_context parameter to build_prompt()
- Parse use_library flag in /api/generate endpoint
- 10 new tests (112 total)

Note: After deploying, delete .spotify_cache on Azure to re-auth with
the new scope.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

test: scorecard event → handicap async pipeline coverage (#45)

* test: add IT for scorecard event → handicap async pipeline

Verifies that creating and deleting a scorecard publishes the
ScorecardEvent and triggers HandicapService.calculate() via the
async ScorecardEventListener. Uses Awaitility to handle async
timing and OutputCaptureExtension to assert log output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add unit test for ScorecardEventListener

Verifies that CREATED and DELETED events both invoke
HandicapService#calculate with the scorecards returned by
ScorecardService#listAll for the event's userId. Calls
scorecardEvent() directly to bypass async machinery.

commit.message:
feat(btc): late-entry high-conviction strategy — 85¢+ in final 1.5min

New strategy: let the market show direction, then ride the favorite.
- Only enter when one side is 85¢+ (market is very confident)
- Only enter in the final 1.5 minutes (direction is established)
- Side picked by market price, not momentum model
- TP $5, SL $8, one trade per market

Logic: buy at 85¢, settles at $1 = 15¢ profit per share.
If wrong (rare at 85¢+), lose 85¢ per share.

Trades wiped, $500 clean start.

Fixes #1898: Rename 8 context-dependent test names in test_base_runner.py to be self-documenting (#1917)

Renamed tests to include method context and behavior so CI output is
readable without knowing the enclosing test class:
- test_stores_config → test_init_stores_config_reference
- test_stores_event_bus → test_init_stores_event_bus_reference
- test_both_present → test_inject_returns_manifest_and_memory_when_both_present
- test_manifest_only → test_inject_returns_manifest_section_when_only_manifest_exists
- test_memory_only → test_inject_returns_memory_section_when_only_digest_exists
- test_neither_present → test_inject_returns_empty_strings_when_no_manifest_or_digest
- test_empty_procs_no_error → test_terminate_with_empty_procs_does_not_raise
- test_truncates_output → test_verify_quality_truncates_long_failure_output

the live connections array on each render.

Add cache-busting headers to force Safari reload

Added cache control meta tags and version query parameters to
force browsers (especially Safari) to reload the new JavaScript
bundles with geometry validation fixes.

This ensures users get the updated code that includes:
- Backend geometry filtering (2-point LineString → Point conversion)
- Frontend fallback validation

feat(tui): add Ansible run summary parser and retry-failed-phases button

Introduces a streaming line parser (AnsibleOutputParser) that extracts
play/task structure and PLAY RECAP counts from Ansible YAML callback
output. The bootstrap run screen now renders a structured summary after
each Ansible step — showing OK/changed/failed/skipped counts and a
numbered list of failed tasks with role, play, and error context.

When failures are detected the parser maps them back to phase IDs via
the playbook manifest and exposes a "Retry Failed" button that re-runs
only the broken phases, keeping the rest of the pipeline intact.

commit.message:
Fix click debouncing suppressing mouse up events (#1084)

## Summary

- Fix mouse up events being suppressed when the preceding mouse down was
debounced, causing window resize and drag operations to malfunction on
subsequent clicks
- Remove unused state machine from ClickDebouncingTransformer

Fixes #912

## Problem

When click debouncing is enabled on a mouse with double-click issues,
window resizing and drag operations malfunction:

- **Window resizing**: After resizing and releasing the mouse button,
clicking elsewhere causes the window to resize to that position
- **File drag & drop**: Files don't drop at the intended destination —
they remain "held" and drop wherever the user clicks next

### Reproduction video



https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/642960dd-b799-4533-9517-6f90edb9a4f8


### Root cause

When a mouse bounces, the debouncing logic correctly ignores the bounced
mouse down, but also suppresses the **subsequent mouse up**. Although
the OS already received a release from the bounce's mouse up (step 2),
the suppression of the real mouse up (step 4) prevents macOS's window
server from properly cleaning up drag/resize state.

**Event flow (before fix):**
```
1. Mouse Down (click/drag)   → passes through    → OS: button pressed
2. Mouse Up   (bounce)       → passes through    → OS: button released
3. Mouse Down (bounce)       → debounced ✓       → OS: no event
4. Mouse Up   (real release) → suppressed ✗      → OS: no event
```

Even though the OS receives a release at step 2, the bounce happens
within milliseconds — too fast for the window server to fully terminate
drag/resize state. The real mouse up at step 4 is needed to properly
clean up, but the old code suppressed it.

As a result, subsequent click/drag interactions (step 5-6) incorrectly
resume the previous resize or drag operation.

> **Note:** This issue occurs when the bounce happens during a click or
short drag where step 3 falls within the debounce timeout. During a long
drag, the interval since step 1 exceeds the timeout, so step 3 is not
debounced and all events pass through normally.

## Fix

Remove the logic that suppresses mouse up events when the preceding
mouse down was debounced. Also remove the now-unused `State` enum and
`state` variable, as they were only used for this suppression logic.

**Event flow (after fix):**
```
1. Mouse Down (click/drag)   → passes through    → OS: button pressed
2. Mouse Up   (bounce)       → passes through    → OS: button released
3. Mouse Down (bounce)       → debounced ✓       → OS: no event
4. Mouse Up   (real release) → passes through ✓  → OS: properly terminates drag/resize state
```

## Test plan

- [x] Manual test: Enable click debouncing → click near window edge →
verify subsequent clicks don't trigger unintended resize
- [x] Manual test: Enable click debouncing → drag window to resize →
verify release works correctly

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Disable webpack worker parallelism to fix Windows ARM crash

Jest worker child process exceptions on /modes/* routes caused by
x64/ARM64 DLL mismatch under emulation. Setting cpus:1 and
parallelism:1 forces single-process compilation.

fix(conversation-browser): Fix race condition in MCP startup

The constructor called initializeBackgroundServices() without awaiting it,
allowing tool calls to proceed before the skeleton cache was loaded.

Fix: Store the initialization promise and await it in the tool call wrapper.
This ensures all tools wait for cache loading before proceeding.

commit.message:
fix: resolve all golangci-lint issues across codebase

Fix ~80 lint issues across 33 files:
- errcheck: add explicit error handling or `_ =` for intentionally ignored errors
- gosec G115: add nolint for safe integer conversions bounded by protocol
- gosec G404: suppress for non-security load balancing random
- gosec G306: tighten file permissions to 0600 where appropriate
- unused: remove unused fields (dhtNode, mu) and function (geminiMockBackend)
- gosimple: simplify loop to append and merge var declaration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix add item: create then update with cells format

The Slack items.create API ignores our fields format, resulting in
untitled items with no status or assignees. Switch to a two-step
approach: create the item first, then immediately update it using
the cells format (column_id + value/select/people) which is proven
to work for drag-drop, rename, and field edits.
